author: jameshe5018
content:
<@586161934425128960> ok so while i'm making changes to fix proposer preferences I'm noticing something. in the prysm implementation we use/eth/v1/validator/prepare_beacon_proposer to keep track of all validators connected to prysm via a cache called tracked validators cache.

the problem is now that we want to switch over the proposer preferences it only submits them for the proposers within a limited set of time. this conflicts with how we determine custody as that sub set is a much smaller set compared to the number connected to the bn

author: jameshe5018
content: wonder if you guys ran into this as well or no

author: jameshe5018
content:
maybe we send all the preferences regardless if they are proposing or not and only broad cast the ones that are proposing? 🤔  just have the api ignore broadcasting on preferences 
or we keep the prepare beacon proposer endpoint?

author: nflaig
content: yes, I believe we use the same api for this but there are other options like `POST /eth/v1/validator/beacon_committee_subscriptions` which should work exactly the same, or you could even use `POST /eth/v2/beacon/pool/attestations` and track attestations over an epoch to determine validator custody, the first option is likly the simplest replacement though
